rows.notes: Very clear centered page title at top of the sheet; ink and lettering are crisp and unambiguous.

Appears as a section-heading directly under the title, centered/indented; letters are legible though final word is slightly abbreviated/uncertain (interpreted as 'Founders'). No aligned monetary figures on this heading line.

rows.description: Bursarys pro Imp: Coll: & West: Dom: H.
rows.notes: Line reads as an item heading/entry for bursaries; no clear monetary figures aligned to the right for this line. Handwriting abbreviations ('pro Imp', 'Coll') required interpretation.
rows.english_description: Bursaries or pension payments relating to the college and an abbreviated Weston/Foundation entry (abbreviated Latin/English).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Bursaries or pension payments relating to the college and an abbreviated Weston/Foundation entry, amount not specified on this line.

rows.description: To Mr Cosserat for Mr Birchinsher's Decrd at Xmas 1726
rows.notes: Amount '4 12 2' is well aligned in the rightmost three columns. Description has some letter uncertainty (surname forms) but context and nearby abbreviations make the reading plausible.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Cosserat connected with Mr Birchinsher’s (deceased or decretal) matter at Christmas 1726.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Cosserat connected with Mr Birchinsher’s matter at Christmas 1726, amounting to £4 12s 2d.

rows.description: To Mr Question for his Decrd at Xmas 1729 and Hm: 1730
rows.notes: Large pounds figure '108' clearly written in the pounds column with '10' and '0' in shillings and pence columns respectively; description is legible enough to identify as payment to 'Mr Question' for the years shown.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Question relating to his entitlement at Christmas 1729 and at the Hilary/Michaelmas period 1730 (two dated payments combined).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Question relating to his entitlement at Christmas 1729 and 1730, amounting to £108 10s 0d.

rows.description: To Mr Bayley for his Debt at Xmas 1726
rows.notes: Amounts '23 8 4' are clearly placed in the three columns. Surname 'Bayley' and phrase 'Debt at Xmas 1726' are reasonably legible.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Bayley for his debt due at Christmas 1726.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Bayley for his debt due at Christmas 1726, amounting to £23 8s 4d.

rows.notes: Second line referring to the same person shows amounts '48 11 2' clearly aligned. 'Decr.' is an abbreviation but context supports reading as 'Decr.' (decree/deceased).
rows.english_description: Payment (continuation) to Mr Bayley for his decretal/decreed amount at Christmas 1727 (same recipient as previous line).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Bayley for his decretal/decreed amount at Christmas 1727, amounting to £48 11s 2d.

rows.description: Allowed Mr Question for Battles due to him in the several years of his Bursarship
rows.notes: Amount '55 18 2' is neatly aligned. The long description is somewhat cramped but key words ('Allowed', 'Mr Question', 'Bursarship') are readable, supporting the interpretation.
rows.english_description: Allowance paid to Mr Question for battels (college charges) owed to him across the years of his bursarship.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Allowance paid to Mr Question for battels owed to him over the several years of his bursarship, amounting to £55 18s 2d.

rows.notes: Center/section heading introducing a grouped set of receipts/payments (bracket on left); clearly separated from previous lines and no amounts on this heading line.

rows.description: Pro Aug: S: New: et Weston
rows.notes: Amounts '21 10 0' are clear in the columns. The description contains several abbreviated place/person names ('S. New.', 'Weston') which are legible but abbreviated, producing slightly reduced confidence.
rows.english_description: Augmentation (top-up grant) relating to South Newington and Weston.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Augmentation relating to South Newington and Weston, amounting to £21 10s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount '17 13 4' clearly visible. Description handwriting is abbreviated; read as 'Ad Walin: Shippend' from the ledger script — some letters uncertain but meaning plausible.
rows.english_description: Receipt described under an abbreviated place or farm name (Walin Shippend) for rent or services.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt from the place listed as Walin Shippend (abbreviated), amounting to £17 13s 4d.

rows.description: To Dunn: Ship: & rest et serv.:
rows.notes: Amounts '25 13 4' readable but ink is slightly lighter here. Description contains abbreviations ('Dunn', 'Ship: & rest') that are moderately ambiguous; chosen reading fits context and bracket grouping.
rows.english_description: Receipt from Dunn for the shippond (or 'ship') and other rents or services.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt from Dunn for the shippond and other services, amounting to £25 13s 4d.

rows.notes: Amounts '26 3 10' are present and within valid ranges. The description has several abbreviations and a surname/place that is difficult ('Chign/Cull'), so transcription of those words is uncertain.
rows.english_description: Rent for a third part of the Chignall/Cull estate (abbreviated place name).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt for the third part of Chignall/Cull (abbreviated), amounting to £26 3s 10d.

rows.description: Ad D: Joh: B: Ship: rest et serv:
rows.notes: Amounts '16 6 8' are aligned and legible. The left initials and abbreviations (D. Joh. B.) are readable but their full expansion is uncertain; reading kept as written.
rows.english_description: Receipt from Dr John B. for shippond and other rents or services (abbreviated name).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt from Dr John B. for shippond and other services, amounting to £16 6s 8d.

rows.notes: Smaller amount '6 11 9' is visible but the pence area has some ink smudge making the last digit slightly unclear; chosen reading '9d' fits the visible shape better than alternatives. Description is heavily abbreviated and uncertain.
rows.english_description: Receipt for one quarter part of Chignall/Cull (abbreviated).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt for the quarter part of Chignall/Cull (abbreviated), amounting to £6 11s 9d.

rows.notes: Amounts '16 18 7' are present though the shillings digit is slightly cramped; place-name 'Wittenham' is readable and matches the letter forms in the image but could be mis-spelled.
rows.english_description: Receipt relating to the rent or dues of Wittenham (abbreviated entry).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt relating to the rent/dues of Wittenham, amounting to £16 18s 7d.

rows.notes: Amount '22 0 0' is clear. The description appears as 'Sol: pro Guineas' (or 'pro Guinea') — the final word reads cleanly as 'Guineas' given the looped 'G' and trailing 's'.
rows.english_description: Receipt described as a sum in guineas (payment or sale in guineas).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt recorded as for guineas, amounting to £22 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Subtotal line 'Summa omnium receptarum' clearly labeled and totals read as '1079. 12. 5' in the three columns; digits well aligned and consistent with column rulings.
Subtraction line 'Solutorum' with amounts '865. 10. 8' is crisp and unambiguous; columns align well with the subtotal above.
Final carried balance reads '214. 1. 9' and sits beneath the subtraction. Phrase is in Latin with abbreviation '2do' (second) in the text; numbers are clear.
First signature line; name is legible in a formal script and clearly a signatory rather than an amount-bearing row.
Signature with an office indicated ('sub-rector'); letters are clear enough to read the surname 'Bayley' corresponding to earlier entries.
Signature and title; the surname is uncertain (transcribed here as 'Douembo' to reflect the visible letter shapes), hence slightly reduced confidence for exact spelling.
Another signatory; the given name 'Jacobus' is clear in Latin form. Surname reading 'Esphard' fits the visible strokes though alternate spellings possible.
Signature is legible and the surname 'Webber' is clearly formed; confident extraction.
Final signature line; 'Johannes Cocke' reads clearly in the image and is distinct from monetary lines.
